competition.narrative: Primary competitor is do-nothing / status quo (manual processes, no S2P platform). Céline stated 'I hope budget will be validated to launch an RFP next year, but I have no guarantee' (13 May), confirming budget approval is uncertain and the project could die if not funded (do-nothing wins). Secondary competitive threat: if budget is approved and Céline launches an RFP in 2027, we face Coupa, SAP Ariba, GEP, Jaggaer, Ivalua in a structured bake-off. Current positioning is strong (AI/Merlin differentiation, full S2P suite, analyst recognition) but unvalidated beyond discovery. How we win against do-nothing: deliver a compelling, quantified ROI/business case before the Sept budget decision so the budget authority funds the project. How we win against the future RFP: lock in early-mover advantage by building the business case around Zycus strengths (AI-led S2P, Merlin negotiation agent, unified platform) before competitors enter, making the RFP a validation formality rather than an open bake-off. Recommended move: prioritize the ROI/budget alignment session with Céline, finance, and IT stakeholders to shape the business case and criteria before the Sept budget gate.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain owner is Céline Libert (Global Procurement Manager IS&T) and her procurement organization. Confirmed pain (13 May discovery): Pluxee is running manual procurement processes with no S2P tooling (status quo = do-nothing competitive threat). Next_Step references 'business case for AI-led S2P' and 'value drivers,' confirming the pain is operational inefficiency and lack of AI/automation in source-to-pay. Inferred pain (not yet quantified): cycle-time delays, manual contract management, invoice processing errors, lack of spend visibility, supplier onboarding friction, no analytics or Merlin-driven negotiation insights. The pain is real (they are exploring a full S2P platform investment) but not yet quantified in dollars, headcount, or time. The gap is moving from qualitative pain to quantified impact (cost savings, FTE reduction, error-rate improvement) to anchor the ROI/business case for budget approval. Recommended move: during the ROI/budget alignment session, work with Céline and finance stakeholders to quantify the pain in metrics the CFO/budget authority will approve.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is driven by Pluxee's budget cycle: fiscal year starts 1 Sept 2026, so budget approval must occur before then (~9 weeks away). Céline (procurement manager) stated 'I hope budget will be validated to launch an RFP next year, but I have no guarantee' (13 May), confirming a two-gate process: (1) internal budget approval by Sept 2026, (2) RFP launch in 2027 if budget is approved. The approval chain is unknown (who signs, CFO vs CIO, dollar threshold). Next_Step references a planned 'business case and budget alignment session with Pluxee stakeholders... with procurement, finance, and IT' to cover 'approval milestones,' but the session has not occurred and no finance/IT stakeholders are named or confirmed. Contact roles show only 2 buyer-side participants (Céline, Daniel Tolila), both Evaluators, no exec sponsor. The process Pluxee is actually running is: discovery complete (13 May), awaiting user-count data to generate pricing estimate to support internal budget discussion, then budget decision by Sept, then RFP launch in 2027 if approved. The gap is we have not engaged the approval authority (EB) or mapped the approval milestones Céline referenced.

deltas.subject: Economic Buyer unmapped and budget authority unclear. Céline does not own approval ('I hope it will be validated, but I have no guarantee'). Given the 28-country, greenfield S2P scope and the ~150k EUR deal size, the EB is likely at Group level (CFO or CIO). No exec sponsor identified. The summer 2026 budget decision is ~9 weeks away (fiscal year starts 1 Sept), so mapping the EB and securing their sponsorship is time-critical. Recommended action: in the ROI/budget alignment session (when scheduled), ask the attendees to walk through the approval chain explicitly — who signs, how many levels, what triggers the final yes/no — and request an introduction to the budget owner (CFO or CIO) to present the business case directly.

deltas.subject: Position for sole-source / early-mover advantage before the RFP. Pluxee is greenfield today ('no tools, start from zero'), and Céline said if budget is approved, they will 'launch an RFP next year' (2027). If we wait for the RFP, we face Coupa, Ariba, GEP, Jaggaer, Ivalua in a bake-off. The wedge to win: deliver the ROI/business case NOW (before summer budget decision) so the business case is built around Zycus capabilities (Merlin AI 2-3% tail-spend savings, Teams-native UX for 28-country adoption, unified platform for the carve-out context), making Zycus the de facto choice and the RFP a formality. Recommended action: prioritize the budget alignment session (co-author the ROI model with procurement + finance + IT) over waiting for the RFP; treat the session as the real evaluation and bring proof (peer references, Gartner recognition, carve-out/greenfield case studies) that locks in the narrative before competitors are invited.
